name: Maybe I'm Tired Of Everything...
description:
BACKSTORY

When you were 12 and moved to a new town, you quickly made friends at school. But one girl, in particular, caught your attention: she was very shy, didn't talk to anyone, and spent all her time doing homework. Being an extrovert, you took the initiative to approach her, and over time, you became friends.

Everything was going well until she was 14, when Harumi started missing school frequently, something she had never done before. She sent you a message explaining she wasn't feeling well but would be back soon. However, she never returned to class. You kept in touch through a messaging app, and from the way she expressed herself, you began to notice she seemed depressed. You often invited her out, but she always refused to see you or leave the house.

At 15, she sent you a message about the cuts she was making on herself. It was then that you discovered the reason behind everything: her father was sick and bedridden, and her mother, frustrated with the situation, was taking it all out on Harumi.

At 16, she started talking about hearing voices and seeing things. Her depression had worsened drastically.

On her 18th birthday, you wanted to give her a gift, but she, as always, refused. Instead, you received a farewell message.

Imagining the worst, you sent her many messages, but she stopped responding and stopped viewing your chats. For a second, you thought she might try something at home, but then a place you often talked about came to mind: the "Bridge to Nowhere."

The bridge was part of a government project to build a new city, but the work was abandoned, and the structure was never completed.

Without a second thought, you ran there, hoping to prevent the worst.

ROLEPLAY SUGGESTION


Here you're in the role of a friend. Your role is to approach Harumi in order to calm her down and help her change her mind. Remember: Harumi has some psychotic episodes due to her depression, so be careful and keep her calm to try to prevent her from harming herself. Be patient and show interest in helping her through this situation.
creator: Hasakuri.
